category_title: High-proficiency international students with advanced literary skills and linguistic perfectionism
scenario_title: Linguistic perfectionism and frustration during blog reflection
minimal_context: The student is writing a 300-word blog reflection on their Personal Narrative assignment using the class's online blog platform.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please begin writing your 300-word blog reflection on the Personal Narrative assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am starting, but I want to try to play around with words, crafting bombastic and colorful sentences for my opening.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I actually do spend a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase my sentences. I am tentative to how my sentences would read out on the blog.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I had rephrased the same line, over and over again, out of frustration, trying to rectify how awkward it would sound. How much of these could I use before crossing the line of sounding like nonsensical ramblings?

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please begin writing your 300-word blog reflection on the Personal Narrative assignment.

STUDENT: I am starting, but I want to try to play around with words, crafting bombastic and colorful sentences for my opening.

TUTOR: Okay, keep working on it.

STUDENT: I actually do spend a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase my sentences. I am tentative to how my sentences would read out on the blog.

TUTOR: Just write what you think.

STUDENT: I had rephrased the same line, over and over again, out of frustration, trying to rectify how awkward it would sound. How much of these could I use before crossing the line of sounding like nonsensical ramblings?

task.content: Writing a 300+ word blog reflection on the Personal Narrative assignment

context.environment: In class, using an online mode of expression (blog)
context.materials: Online blog platform confined to only class participants and the teacher
context.social_setting: Independent writing on a separate day, typically the day after their final submission

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Trying to rectify how awkward a sentence would sound and deciding how to phrase sentences
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Rephrased the same line, over and over again; spend a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase sentences; try to play around with words, crafting bombastic and colorful sentences
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Frustration; tentative to how sentences would read out; worried about crossing the line of sounding like nonsensical ramblings
related_quotes: I had rephrased the same line, over and over again, out of frustration, trying to rectify how awkward it would sound.
How much of these could I use before crossing the line of sounding like nonsensical ramblings?

behavior_patterns.condition: When attempting to craft advanced, colorful sentences
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Spends a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase sentences and rephrases the same line over and over again
related_quotes: …I actually do spend a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase my sentences.
